skill.name: prompt-engineer
skill.description: Transforms rough prompts into clear, structured prompts using established frameworks such as RTF, RISEN, RODES, RACE, STAR, and GROW.
skill.summary: Optimizes AI prompts by selecting and blending prompt frameworks for the user's task.
skill.author: Eric Andrade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but manual review found them in markdown examples, prompt templates, install instructions, and relative documentation links. No executable scripts, hidden runtime behavior, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection attempt was found in README.md or SKILL.md. The skill is documentation-only and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Documentation Shell Examples Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are markdown code fences and usage examples, not code that the skill executes. They show sample invocations and generated prompt text for users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside markdown examples and contain no runnable skill script or tool call. The repository files in scope are README.md and SKILL.md only.

low_findings.title: Installation URL Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL appears in a git clone installation example. It is user-facing documentation and does not cause automatic network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is shown in a fenced bash block under installation steps. There is no executable file or instruction that silently sends data to this URL.

low_findings.title: Configuration Paths Misclassified as Filesystem Access
low_findings.description: The hidden file and directory findings are setup instructions for a user's Copilot configuration. They do not read, write, or exfiltrate files during skill use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear in installation documentation and configuration examples. Manual review found no code path that accesses hidden files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings are relative documentation links to nearby markdown files. They are not used for file access in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown links in a Learn More section. They do not form a runtime path traversal vector.

low_findings.title: Prompt Framework Terms Misclassified as Blockers
low_findings.description: The blocker findings map to natural-language prompt framework descriptions and examples. They do not contain cryptographic implementation, reconnaissance logic, or malicious instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a prompt-engineering framework guide. I found no weak cryptographic algorithm usage, system reconnaissance command, or network reconnaissance operation.

content.user_title: Optimize AI Prompts with Proven Frameworks
content.value_statement: Users often know what they want but struggle to write a precise AI prompt. This skill turns rough requests into structured prompts for Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other assistants.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes a user's rough prompt to identify task type, complexity, domain, and missing context.
Selects from 11 named prompt frameworks based on the task requirements.
Combines multiple frameworks when a prompt needs both structure and reasoning support.
Asks up to three clarifying questions when critical details are missing.
Generates self-contained prompts with role, task, context, constraints, and output format.
Adapts output language based on the user's original prompt language.
limitations: It improves prompt wording but does not verify facts in the final prompt.
It does not execute commands, call APIs, or inspect project files.
It may need user clarification when the original request is too broad.
It does not customize the underlying framework definitions beyond the provided instructions.

use_cases.title: Turn a vague coding request into a usable prompt
use_cases.description: Convert a short software idea into a prompt with role, requirements, constraints, examples, and expected output.

use_cases.title: Structure business and product analysis prompts
use_cases.description: Create prompts that guide an assistant through audience, context, decision criteria, trade-offs, and final recommendations.

use_cases.title: Create repeatable writing and learning prompts
use_cases.description: Refine broad writing or study goals into prompts with clear scope, format, evaluation criteria, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Simple Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this prompt: [paste your rough prompt]. Keep the final prompt short, clear, and ready to use.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Task-Specific Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prompt for: [describe the task]. Include the role, task, important context, constraints, and output format.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Complex Project Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Refine this project idea into a detailed AI prompt: [describe the project]. Include assumptions to check, steps, deliverables, examples, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Advanced Reasoning Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced analysis, architecture, or decision support
prompt_templates.prompt: Transform this request into an advanced reasoning prompt: [paste request]. Blend structure, step-by-step analysis, validation criteria, risks, and a final decision format.

output_examples.input: Improve this prompt: help me write Python code.
output_examples.output: The skill asks what type of Python code is needed, then returns a focused prompt for that use case with role, requirements, and output format.

output_examples.input: Create prompt for a recommendation system using purchase history and market trends.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a structured machine learning design prompt with objective, data sources, algorithm considerations, evaluation metrics, and roadmap.

output_examples.input: Transform into prompt: I want to improve my process.
output_examples.output: The skill asks targeted clarification questions, then produces a practical prompt for process diagnosis, options, and measurable improvement.

best_practices: Provide the goal, audience, constraints, and desired output format when available.
Answer clarification questions with concrete choices so the generated prompt has enough context.
Review the generated prompt before use and add private or domain-specific details yourself.
anti_patterns: Do not expect the skill to research current facts or validate source material.
Do not use it as a substitute for domain review on legal, medical, financial, or safety work.
Do not provide secrets or credentials in a prompt that will be reused with external tools.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It rewrites rough user requests into structured prompts using established prompt-engineering frameworks.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is written for Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and similar conversational AI tools.

faq.question: Do I need to choose a prompt framework?
faq.answer: No. The skill selects a framework based on task type, complexity, and available context.

faq.question: Will it ask follow-up questions?
faq.answer: Only when critical information is missing. The skill limits clarification to a small number of questions.

faq.question: Can it generate prompts in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. It can match the user's input language when the skill instructions support that language.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access files?
faq.answer: No. Manual review found only markdown instructions and examples, with no executable runtime behavior.
